channel.title: Terms on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Terms on emsenn.net

item.title: Weak messianic power
item.description: Weak messianic power is the term Walter Benjamin uses, in the second of his “Theses on the Philosophy of History” (1940), for the power each living generation possesses to recognize a claim from past generations on the present and to act on that claim. The past has expectations of us — the dead asked things of those who would come after them, and we are those who have come after. The power to fulfill or refuse those expectations is weak (it is not sovereign, it is not the messianic in any redemptive sense) but it is nevertheless a power. “The past carries with it a temporal index by which it is referred to redemption. There is a secret agreement between past generations and the present one. Our coming was expected on earth. Like every generation that preceded us, we have been endowed with a weak messianic power, a power on which the past has a claim.”
